title: Crispy Moroccan Fish Stir-fry
description: A tangy and crispy Moroccan dish featuring harissa spice for a weeknight meal that's perfect for pescatarian diets.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Season fish fillets with salt and a pinch of cayenne pepper.
Dip fish in flour mixture until coated evenly. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic, onion, carrots, and bell peppers; cook for about 5 minutes or until vegetables start to soften.
Remove from heat and add harissa paste; mix well for another minute.
Return fish to the pan with vegetables and stir everything together. Season with salt as needed.
Transfer the mixture into an oven-safe baking dish, ensuring all pieces are coated evenly in the spices and vegetables.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15 minutes or until the edges of the fish start to crisp up and become golden brown.
Serve hot with a sprinkle of fresh parsley over top.
